description: Larnaca, Cyprus's oldest continuously inhabited city, stretches along a sun-drenched coastline where ancient history and modern beach life coexist. The city's palm-lined Finikoudes promenade, with its golden sand beaches and crystal-clear waters, serves as the heart of daily life, where locals and visitors alike gather to enjoy the Mediterranean sun. Just inland, the salt lake, a seasonal home to flocks of pink flamingos, reflects the city's natural beauty, while the medieval fort stands as a reminder of Larnaca's strategic importance through the ages. The Church of Saint Lazarus, with its intricate Byzantine architecture, speaks to the city's deep religious heritage, while the nearby Hala Sultan Tekke mosque represents Cyprus's multicultural past. The ancient ruins of Kition, dating back to the 13th century BC, lie scattered throughout the modern city, offering glimpses into its Phoenician and Mycenaean origins. Larnaca's laid-back atmosphere, combined with its rich history and stunning coastal setting, makes it a perfect introduction to Cyprus's diverse cultural and natural heritage.
